topNews.title: LLMorphism: When humans come to see themselves as language models
topNews.rawTextSnippet: LLMorphism: When humans come to see themselves as language models
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A new concept called "LLMorphism" suggests humans are beginning to perceive themselves similarly to language models.  
• Why it matters: This shift in perception could impact human communication and identity in the digital age.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can explore products and services that enhance human-AI interaction and self-perception.

topNews.title: The left-wing case for AI

research.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
research.rawTextSnippet: Modern Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures allocate expert capacity through a rigid per-layer rule: each transformer layer owns a separate expert set. This convention couples depth scaling with linear expert-parameter growth and assumes that every layer needs isolated expert capacity. However, re…

research.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
research.rawTextSnippet: Many deployments must compare candidate language models for safety before a labeled benchmark exists for the relevant language, sector, or regulatory regime. We formalize this setting as benchmarkless comparative safety scoring and specify the contract under which a scenario-based audit can be inter…

research.title: Beyond Negative Rollouts: Positive-Only Policy Optimization with Implicit Negative Gradients
research.rawTextSnippet: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), due to the deterministic verification, becomes a dominant paradigm for enhancing the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs). The community witnesses the rapid change from the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to Group Relative Policy…

research.title: Patch2Vuln: Agentic Reconstruction of Vulnerabilities from Linux Distribution Binary Patches
research.rawTextSnippet: Security updates create a short but important window in which defenders and attackers can compare vulnerable and patched software. research.rawTextSnippet: For artistic applications, video generation requires fine-grained control over both performance and cinematography, i.e., the actor's motion and the camera trajectory. dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, the emergence of LLMorphism highlights a growing trend where individuals increasingly identify with language models, potentially reshaping communication dynamics in the workplace. Meanwhile, Google's Gemini API has introduced multimodal file search capabilities, enhancing data retrieval efficiency for businesses. On the ideological front, a left-wing perspective on AI advocates for equitable technology deployment, emphasizing its potential to address social disparities. Notably, Anthropic and OpenAI are collaborating on joint ventures aimed at delivering advanced enterprise AI services, signaling a competitive push in the market for tailored AI solutions. These developments underscore the necessity for businesses to adapt to rapidly evolving AI technologies to maintain a competitive edge.
